summary: WIN-010 c9 defense-neutralization (advocate defense survives 3/5 on Kim & Chang ≤30% citation). Curmudgeon option (b) — reframe parenthetical as conservative upper bound in absence of paper-tabulated attenuation factor — implemented as H1 patch. Bundled with three persisting minor holes (c6→c9): H2 parallel-drift softening, H3 0.1%/1.8% → 0.6%/2.78% propagation to detail_evidence (dvt already patched today), H4 'any smooth monotonic H(r)' → 'a broad family ... fitted to the same calibration targets'. All four substring targets verified 1 occurrence before filing.
detail: ISS-1496 (severity=major, category=defense) flagged that WIN-010's newly-added ≤30% out-of-band suppression bound cites Kim & Chang (2018)'s 'published attenuation envelope' but the paper does not tabulate a specific attenuation factor for stations outside the 30°–50° CGM band. The c9 advocate_mode defense survives at 3/5 with attack: 'your number is pulled from a paper that does not contain it.' The curmudgeon's preemptive_recommendation offered three options: (a) locate a specific K&C figure supporting the factor (strongest if doable); (b) reframe as conservative upper bound with explicit acknowledgement that the paper does not tabulate it; (c) upgrade to within-cluster-variance comparison. Option (b) is selected as fastest/cheapest per curmudgeon's own recommendation and preserves the binary-classifier disclosure added in ISS-1485. The expansion file specifies four substring replacements with verified 1-occurrence targets; no edits to claim, finding, verdict, TLDRs, code_analysis, or detail_extra; operative verdict 'Std Model Explains' unchanged. Post-patch c10 should find defense_survives ≤ 2/5 on this line of attack.

patches_summary.hole: c6→c9 H3 minor persisting (dvt already patched today)

assigned_exp_collision_note: ISS-1496.assigned_exp field says 'EXP-240' but EXP-240 is already in use (EXP-240-WIN069-circuity-citation-retraction-iss1487-iss1488.json, Apr 23). Separately, the expansion-tracker's next_id was stale at 238 while disk held EXP-238 through EXP-242. I allocated EXP-243 and bumped next_id to 244. Decider: please update ISS-1496.assigned_exp to 'EXP-243' (or treat this findings_file as authoritative). A second integrity proposal flags the broader tracker/disk drift for audit.
cross_win_consistency_flag: Post-patch, WIN-010 continues to carry the most-developed pre-registration language among WIN-010/025/068. Cross-WIN propagation is deliberately deferred; see expansion's cross_win_consistency_flag.
do_not: Do NOT apply the four patches independently across multiple decider runs — they are tightly coupled (H1 introduces citation-honest framing; H2/H3/H4 are the batched persisting-minor cleanup per c9 curmudgeon guidance).

Do NOT propagate the ≤30% bound language to WIN-025/WIN-068 as part of this patch — those are past eclipses and the ≤30% is a prospective 2026 test; cross-WIN propagation is out-of-scope-deliberately.
Do NOT touch claim, finding, verdict, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_extra, or code_analysis on WIN-010 — all patches are scoped to detail_verdict_text and detail_evidence only.
Do NOT assume the assigned_exp collision is safe to ignore — at least fix the ISS-1496 metadata before marking resolved, so future audits of assigned_exp don't point at the WIN-069 file.
